hyperedges.label: Three-phase transfer approval workflow (citizen initiates, officer verifies, admin approves)
Admin approval side effects: close ownership, rechain hashes, insert OwnershipHistory, rebuild Merkle tree
Two-step record verification: hash recompute then Merkle proof
Two-stage Land Transfer Approval Workflow (Citizen -> Officer -> Admin)
Record Integrity Engine (hashing, Merkle proofs, hash chain)
Three-tier Architecture (Web tier -> Spring Boot app -> Persistence/Storage)
Three-Phase Ownership Transfer Workflow (Citizen -> Officer -> Admin)

nodes.label: Merkle Tree Verification
nodes.rationale: Chosen to give tamper-proof record storage with O(log n) verification via proof paths instead of rehashing all records

nodes.label: SHA-256 Hash Chain Integrity
nodes.rationale: Sequential chaining of record hashes (previous_record_hash links) makes records immutable: editing any record breaks all downstream hashes

nodes.label: Ownership Transfer Workflow
nodes.rationale: Three-stage Citizen initiate -> Officer verify -> Admin approve flow separates initiation, verification, and final approval authority across roles

nodes.label: S3-Compatible Object Storage Config
nodes.rationale: S3-compatible endpoint chosen so RustFS in Docker, MinIO, or AWS S3 are interchangeable; documents live in object storage, not on the container disk

nodes.label: Test Config (H2 in-memory, PostgreSQL mode)
nodes.rationale: H2 in PostgreSQL compatibility mode with ddl-auto create-drop and Flyway disabled, for fast isolated tests without a real database

nodes.label: RustFS Object Storage Service (lrms-storage)
nodes.rationale: Ports 9000/9001 published to the host so a backend running outside Compose can still reach object storage at localhost:9000

nodes.label: TransferStatus Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: ADMIN_APPROVED is terminal and irreversible (ownership closed, chain re-linked, Merkle tree rebuilt). CANCELLED exists in the enum but no controller endpoint currently sets it - designed but unimplemented transition

nodes.label: Activity Diagram - Create Record & Hashing
nodes.rationale: record_hash = SHA-256 over kitta, owner, area, location, type, timestamp (canonicalised to ms) and previous record's hash - timestamp truncation ensures deterministic re-hashing

nodes.label: Layered Backend Architecture
nodes.rationale: Security (JwtAuthFilter/SecurityConfig) -> Controllers -> Services -> Repositories layering isolates the cryptographic integrity engine (merkle package) behind LandRecordIntegrityService

nodes.label: Frontend Generation Prompt (Nepali civic design)
nodes.rationale: Design must feel official, modern, and distinctly Nepali (deep crimson, disciplined blue, paper neutrals) while avoiding tourist cliches and generic AI dashboard patterns; post-login screen is an operational command center, not a landing page
